narratives.evidence_quote: Estudos apontaram que a crise climática pode extinguir 90% dos mamíferos típicos da Caatinga e provocar perdas significativas na flora em 99% do bioma até 2060
narratives.reasoning: This quote amplifies fears about the consequences of climate change on the Caatinga bioma, stating that 90% of its typical mammals may be extinct and 99% of its flora may be lost by 2060, which is a catastrophic scenario.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Doomsday scenarios for humans
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Estudos apontaram que a crise climática pode extinguir 90% dos mamíferos típicos da Caatinga e provocar perdas significativas na flora em 99% do bioma até 2060
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote presents a catastrophic scenario as a result of climate change, specifically stating that 90% of typical mammals in the Caatinga and significant portions of its flora could be lost by 2060, which aligns with the definition of presenting intense, catastrophic scenarios due to climate change.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Amplifying existing fears of global warming
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Já foram perdidos 34 milhões de hectares – área equivalente ao estado de Goiás – dos 82,6 milhões de hectares da Caatinga, 40% do seu território
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote amplifies existing fears of global warming by highlighting the significant loss of area in the Caatinga, which can be linked to the impacts of climate change, thus speculating on side effects to spread panic about the warming of the Earth's surface and atmosphere.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Earth will be uninhabitable soon
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A Caatinga é o único dos 6 biomas do território brasileiro que é 100% nacional. Mas, apesar disso, historicamente, tem recebido pouca atenção por parte das políticas públicas de preservação. E isso reflete-se tanto no que se prevê para o bioma no futuro, como efeito das alterações climáticas, mas, principalmente, no seu presente
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that due to lack of attention and preservation efforts, combined with the effects of climate change, the future of the Caatinga and potentially the Earth could become uninhabitable, aligning with the narrative that the Earth will become uninhabitable soon due to climate change.
